raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1414 aims to protect consumers against predatory debt collection lawsuits by requiring debt collectors to produce a written contract.",
    "Common counts in debt collection cases allow evasion of modern consumer protection standards and can cause extreme hardship for low-income families.",
    "AB 1414 is intended to end shortcuts and special treatment for sophisticated debt collectors.",
    "The bill increases the chances that financially vulnerable consumers will be treated with dignity in court rather than as mere cogs in a debt collection system.",
    "Creditors, debt collectors, and debt buyers must now prove the existence of a contract for an alleged debt before obtaining judgments and seizing wages and bank accounts.",
    "A 2017 Consumer Financial Protection Bureau report indicates that 29% of white respondents have been contacted about a debt in collection, compared to 44% of people of color.",
    "AB 1414 would prohibit the use of common counts in consumer debt collection cases for obligations incurred on or after July 1, 2024.",
    "The definition of consumer debt in AB 1414 specifies that it must be based on a transaction where the obligation to pay appears on the face of a note or in a written contract."
  ]
}
```
